本单元入口
整套首页
unit_20 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_20

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 433 全部题目 8 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)__________ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)__________ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)__________ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)__________ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)__________ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)__________ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　hampered; disparate; impediment to
defineexpediteintegrity
define: standard unit headword | expedite: standard unit headword | integrity: standard unit headword
#2 SE
Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.
答案：A, B　manifest; reveal
groundmanifestundercut
ground: standard unit headword | manifest: standard unit headword | undercut: standard unit headword
#3 TC SINGLE
In his youth the naturalist and artist James Audubon was given to __________ glamorous tales about himself: he falsely claimed to have studied under a renowned French painter and hinted that he was the heir apparent to the French throne.In his youth the naturalist and artist James Audubon was given to __________ glamorous tales about himself: he falsely claimed to have studied under a renowned French painter and hinted that he was the heir apparent to the French throne.
答案：E　concocting
renownedstudied
renowned: standard unit headword | studied: standard unit headword
#4 TC TRIPLE
Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　autonomous; a figurehead; conform to
presumereconcile
presume: standard unit headword | reconcile: standard unit headword
#5 TC SINGLE
The Internet is an eternally unfinished monument to __________, a text that aspires to be as big and multifarious as the world it describes.The Internet is an eternally unfinished monument to __________, a text that aspires to be as big and multifarious as the world it describes.
答案：A　inclusion
promotionabstraction
promotion: standard unit headword | abstraction: standard unit headword
#6 TC DOUBLE
The school system’s modest plan for curriculum improvement has (i)__________ local educators: some call it (ii)__________ effort, while others say it is a pragmatic approach given the complexity of the task.The school system’s modest plan for curriculum improvement has (i)__________ local educators: some call it (ii)__________ effort, while others say it is a pragmatic approach given the complexity of the task.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　divided; halfhearted
halfheartedpragmatic
halfhearted: standard unit headword | pragmatic: standard unit headword
#7 SE
For all the __________ the new CEO has received from the press recently, her staff have a decidedly less rosy view of her.For all the __________ the new CEO has received from the press recently, her staff have a decidedly less rosy view of her.
答案：A, B　encomiums; tributes
for allpublicity
for all: standard unit headword | publicity: standard unit headword
#8 TC TRIPLE
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
congestionairoutrage
congestion: standard unit headword | outrage: standard unit headword

Although Professor Pearson's colleagues often complained that he was (i) __________, his friends were quick to defend him from this charge of (ii) __________.Although Professor Pearson's colleagues often complained that he was (i) __________, his friends were quick to defend him from this charge of (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　mercurial; inconstancy
mercurialgarrulous
mercurial: standard unit headword | garrulous: standard unit headword
#10 SE
If giant x-ray flares churn circumstellar disks enough to keep newborn planets, such as Earth once was, from spiraling into their suns, it would be an ironic twist on our conception of x-ray flares as __________.If giant x-ray flares churn circumstellar disks enough to keep newborn planets, such as Earth once was, from spiraling into their suns, it would be an ironic twist on our conception of x-ray flares as __________.
答案：A, E　dangerous; perilous
ephemeralcircumstellar
ephemeral: standard unit headword | circumstellar: standard unit headword
#11 TC TRIPLE
For many years Cole experienced (i)__________ his professional circle. He was a (ii)__________ figure in the Middle East Studies Association of North America, editing for five years its flagship publication, and in 2004 he was even elected the association's incoming president. But because his research focused on highly (iii)__________ aspects of the eighteenth-and-nineteenth century Middle East, he was unlikely to achieve any sort of public acclaim.For many years Cole experienced (i)__________ his professional circle. He was a (ii)__________ figure in the Middle East Studies Association of North America, editing for five years its flagship publication, and in 2004 he was even elected the association's incoming president. But because his research focused on highly (iii)__________ aspects of the eighteenth-and-nineteenth century Middle East, he was unlikely to achieve any sort of public acclaim.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　anonymity outside; leading; esoteric
disputeacclaim
dispute: standard unit headword | acclaim: standard unit headword
#12 SE
Although the insistence on balancing spending against tax revenues has contributed to the economy’s stagnation, unfortunately, the government does not seem likely to __________ this rigid policy.Although the insistence on balancing spending against tax revenues has contributed to the economy’s stagnation, unfortunately, the government does not seem likely to __________ this rigid policy.
答案：D, F　repudiate; recant
publicizerecant
publicize: standard unit headword | recant: standard unit headword

It is a paradox of the Victorians that they are both __________ and, throughout the empire, cosmopolitan.It is a paradox of the Victorians that they are both __________ and, throughout the empire, cosmopolitan.
答案：B　insular
cosmopolitan
cosmopolitan: standard unit headword
#14 TC DOUBLE
Despite the scathing precision with which she satirizes the lives of the social aspirants and moneyed folk, the writer appears to (i)__________ being part of the world she presents as so (ii)__________.Despite the scathing precision with which she satirizes the lives of the social aspirants and moneyed folk, the writer appears to (i)__________ being part of the world she presents as so (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　relish; insufferable
scathing
scathing: standard unit headword
#15 TC TRIPLE
Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　alarming; reassuring; unexceptional
reassurecontext
reassure: standard unit headword | context: standard unit headword
#16 TC DOUBLE
People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentments if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentments if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　clandestine; open
resolve
resolve: standard unit headword
#17 TC SINGLE
With the numerous opponents of the controversial new taxation measure in such a fury, anyone who publicly advocated the measure did not fail to meet with __________ usage.With the numerous opponents of the controversial new taxation measure in such a fury, anyone who publicly advocated the measure did not fail to meet with __________ usage.
答案：B　severe
usage
usage: standard unit headword
#18 SE

答案：D, E　belie; mask
clot

And though audiences were sometimes unsettled when they found themselves thrust into the midst of the action, on balance they saw this as adding to the (iii) __________ of the performance.The theater presented directors and designers with a remarkably (i) __________ space, since the placement of the stage and of the audience could be (ii) __________ the needs of the production. And though audiences were sometimes unsettled when they found themselves thrust into the midst of the action, on balance they saw this as adding to the (iii) __________ of the performance.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　plastic; dictated by; excitement

#20 TC DOUBLE
Scientists once said that cosmology was the field where the ratio of theory to data was (i)__________: there was an abundance of theories but almost no data. Recently, however, that ratio has flipped. A huge and ever-increasing amount of data has (ii)__________ all theories but one.Scientists once said that cosmology was the field where the ratio of theory to data was (i)__________: there was an abundance of theories but almost no data. Recently, however, that ratio has flipped. A huge and ever-increasing amount of data has (ii)__________ all theories but one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　nearly infinite; eliminated
flip
flip: standard unit headword
#21 TC SINGLE
Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of __________, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of __________, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.
答案：A　synthesis
strand
strand: standard unit headword
#22 SE
Because chemistry’s position as one of the natural sciences has long seemed __________, historians have generally treated the foundation of chemical professorship as an inevitable component of the progression of universities.Because chemistry’s position as one of the natural sciences has long seemed __________, historians have generally treated the foundation of chemical professorship as an inevitable component of the progression of universities.
答案：A, F　manifest; self-evident
self-evidentmanifest

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　immersed in; radicalism
air
air: stem: target sense: manner/atmosphere or public expression

The science community’s perennial lament over inadequate budgets has come to seem increasingly __________, because government support for science and engineering has never been greater.The science community’s perennial lament over inadequate budgets has come to seem increasingly __________, because government support for science and engineering has never been greater.
答案：E　disingenuous
perennial
perennial: standard unit headword

Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)__________ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. The public loved souvenirs; the critics (ii)__________ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)__________ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)__________ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)__________ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　commercial; decried; sincere effort
integritysouvenir
integrity: standard unit headword | souvenir: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent
truculent

Blank 2
答案：A, F　disinclination; callow

答案：C　demise
notwithstanding

#30 TC TRIPLE
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event
return
return: standard unit headword
#31 TC SINGLE
By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not __________ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist pleases, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not __________ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist pleases, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.
答案：D　a passive
entity
entity: standard unit headword

Blank 2

答案：B, E　a hodgepodge; a patchwork

The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce
spate
spate: standard unit headword

Blank 3

Blank 2

Listening to music in this medium often generates anxiety fulfillment: no sooner has one experience begun than the thought of what else is out there intrudes. Blank 2

Anthony Kenny states that although his A New History of Western Philosophy is intended for undergraduates, he aimed to write it in a lighthearted manner that will give (i)__________ to those who read the history "not for curricular purposes but for their own enjoyment". His book is certainly enlightening, and although the bibliography includes technical works liable to (ii)__________ the newcomer, the work is written at a level that generally (iii)__________ between elementary and advanced.Anthony Kenny states that although his A New History of Western Philosophy is intended for undergraduates, he aimed to write it in a lighthearted manner that will give (i)__________ to those who read the history "not for curricular purposes but for their own enjoyment". His book is certainly enlightening, and although the bibliography includes technical works liable to (ii)__________ the newcomer, the work is written at a level that generally (iii)__________ between elementary and advanced.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　pleasure; baffle; strikes a good balance
enlighten

答案：A　tractable
abstract

#49 SE
One of the peculiarities of humans is that we irrationally gravitate to the predictable and avoid risk; whatever the reasons for this __________, it is hardly a sound basis for dealing with complex, long-term problems.One of the peculiarities of humans is that we irrationally gravitate to the predictable and avoid risk; whatever the reasons for this __________, it is hardly a sound basis for dealing with complex, long-term problems.
答案：B, D　predilection; proclivity
proclivity

Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　maximize; reducing; added
forage
forage: standard unit headword

答案：C　egalitarian

Since the field of quantum mechanics is often considered to be (i) __________, it was surprising to find it attracts so much (ii) __________ interest.Since the field of quantum mechanics is often considered to be (i) __________, it was surprising to find it attracts so much (ii) __________ interest.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　abstruse; general
abstruse

#53 SE
In the midst of so many trenchant articles, anything as __________ as this essay is bound to stand out as an anomaly.In the midst of so many trenchant articles, anything as __________ as this essay is bound to stand out as an anomaly.
答案：A, B　vapid; bland
trenchant
trenchant: standard unit headword
#54 TC TRIPLE
Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)__________: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)__________: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from
overshadow
overshadow: standard unit headword

Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating in the process.Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating in the process.
答案：B　addiction to
vexation
vexation: standard unit headword

In our daily lives, we often (i) __________ our separate identities: you can have one identity at work and another online, for example. Such (ii) __________ disappear in certain circumstances, however, resulting in a cross-pollination of our different selves.In our daily lives, we often (i) __________ our separate identities: you can have one identity at work and another online, for example. Such (ii) __________ disappear in certain circumstances, however, resulting in a cross-pollination of our different selves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　compartmentalize; boundaries
compartmentalize

An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematic formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematic formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.
答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar

interdependent: standard unit headword

Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious
insurmountable
insurmountable: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　confronted difficulty; elongated

The actor's performance was so absurdly __________ that Gwen felt a little ashamed to have to resort to tissues in the final scene.The actor's performance was so absurdly __________ that Gwen felt a little ashamed to have to resort to tissues in the final scene.
答案：D　maudlin
proficient
proficient: standard unit headword

Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not __________ his moderate political record.Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not __________ his moderate political record.
答案：B, F　conform to; square with

Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt
unkempt
unkempt: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　abundance; rapacious depletion

答案：B　a panacea

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　expensive; useless; serve no practical purpose

答案：B, F　dissemblance; duplicity

Blank 2
答案：A, E　fully endorse; venal

A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
return
return: standard unit headword
#106 SE
Although in his new book he tends to repeat himself like a __________ uncle, McHughen makes a persuasive case for the safety of tinkering with genes to create new foods.Although in his new book he tends to repeat himself like a __________ uncle, McHughen makes a persuasive case for the safety of tinkering with genes to create new foods.
答案：D, E　garrulous; loquacious
garrulous
garrulous: standard unit headword
#107 TC SINGLE
The painter’s problem, like that of an author whose early literary masterpiece exhausts the themes it embodies, is how to __________ his first highly acclaimed efforts with works of comparable significance and presence.The painter’s problem, like that of an author whose early literary masterpiece exhausts the themes it embodies, is how to __________ his first highly acclaimed efforts with works of comparable significance and presence.
答案：E　follow
acclaim
acclaim: standard unit headword
#108 TC DOUBLE
In science education, it is important to differentiate between inaccurate ideas that are conceptually (i)__________ and understandings that are inaccurate, and yet can (ii)__________ learning of more sophisticated understandings. The former are simply wrong, the latter can be seen as incomplete, overly simplistic, or tied to only a few limited contexts.In science education, it is important to differentiate between inaccurate ideas that are conceptually (i)__________ and understandings that are inaccurate, and yet can (ii)__________ learning of more sophisticated understandings. The former are simply wrong, the latter can be seen as incomplete, overly simplistic, or tied to only a few limited contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　unproductive; foster
context
context: standard unit headword
#109 SE
In sharp contrast to the novel’s scenic realism and precisely characterized figure is its persistent philosophical __________.In sharp contrast to the novel’s scenic realism and precisely characterized figure is its persistent philosophical __________.
答案：B, C　abstraction; generality
abstraction
abstraction: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)__________ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)__________—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)__________ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)__________ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)__________—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)__________ force in the development and production of a movie.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　enduring; profit mongers; creative
ground
ground: standard unit headword
#111 TC SINGLE
A new television documentary focuses on one of the prime ministers defining contradictions, portraying her as a woman who cultivated an image of __________ but who liked to live grandly.A new television documentary focuses on one of the prime ministers defining contradictions, portraying her as a woman who cultivated an image of __________ but who liked to live grandly.
答案：B　abstemiousness
define
define: standard unit headword
#112 TC DOUBLE
Psychologists note that in making purchase decisions, consumers often unconsciously use mental shortcuts, such as familiarity with a brand name to (i) __________ routine problems efficiently. Thus, part of what companies aim for developing a brand is very specifically to (ii) __________ consumers' conscious thought.Psychologists note that in making purchase decisions, consumers often unconsciously use mental shortcuts, such as familiarity with a brand name to (i) __________ routine problems efficiently. Thus, part of what companies aim for developing a brand is very specifically to (ii) __________ consumers' conscious thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　resolve; circumvent
resolve
resolve: standard unit headword
#113 SE
Publicity around the celebrities’ donations to charity is often greeted with cynicism, but a study of the celebrities’ donation shows that they do __________ other donation.Publicity around the celebrities’ donations to charity is often greeted with cynicism, but a study of the celebrities’ donation shows that they do __________ other donation.
答案：C, D　draw; elicit
publicity
publicity: standard unit headword
#114 TC TRIPLE
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
dispute
dispute: standard unit headword
#115 TC SINGLE
The chairman, faced with the need to forge a consensus on a number of proposals, acknowledged that it would be difficult to reconcile the push for a radical overhaul with the stance of those who want __________ change.The chairman, faced with the need to forge a consensus on a number of proposals, acknowledged that it would be difficult to reconcile the push for a radical overhaul with the stance of those who want __________ change.
答案：A　deliberate
reconcile
reconcile: standard unit headword
#116 TC DOUBLE
As a longtime fan of the fashion photography Web site, I wasn't sure if I wanted to see its images presented in a book, with all of the (i) __________ that implies. What I've always enjoyed about the photos is their (ii) __________: they are disposable snapshots of what's going on in the world of style right now.As a longtime fan of the fashion photography Web site, I wasn't sure if I wanted to see its images presented in a book, with all of the (i) __________ that implies. What I've always enjoyed about the photos is their (ii) __________: they are disposable snapshots of what's going on in the world of style right now.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　permanency; ephemeral
ephemeral
ephemeral: standard unit headword
#117 SE
The professor’s habitual air of __________ was a misleading front, concealing amazing reserves of patience and a deep commitment to his students’ learning.The professor’s habitual air of __________ was a misleading front, concealing amazing reserves of patience and a deep commitment to his students’ learning.
答案：B, F　irascibility; exasperation
air
air: stem: target sense: manner/atmosphere or public expression
#118 TC TRIPLE
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)__________ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)__________ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)__________.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)__________ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)__________ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data
studied
studied: standard unit headword
#119 TC DOUBLE
Gelles urges readers of Abigail Adams' letters to consider their (i) __________: in Adams' era women were denied a public persona separate from that of their husbands. That said, Gelles then (ii) __________ to promote Adams from a writer of private letters to a public figure, arguing that she was a significant force for change.Gelles urges readers of Abigail Adams' letters to consider their (i) __________: in Adams' era women were denied a public persona separate from that of their husbands. That said, Gelles then (ii) __________ to promote Adams from a writer of private letters to a public figure, arguing that she was a significant force for change.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　context; strains
context
context: standard unit headword
#120 TC SINGLE
His __________ speaking style notwithstanding, William Perkins has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.His __________ speaking style notwithstanding, William Perkins has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.
答案：E　vehement
notwithstanding
notwithstanding: standard unit headword
#121 SE
The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would __________ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would __________ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.
答案：A, B　combine; meld
entity
entity: standard unit headword
#122 TC TRIPLE
Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　presented; defining features of; propose
define
define: standard unit headword
#123 TC DOUBLE
Scientists said that cosmology was the field where the ratio of theory to data was (i) __________: there was an abundance of theories, but almost no data. Recently, however, that ratio has flipped. A huge and ever-increasing amount of data has (ii) __________ all theories but one.Scientists said that cosmology was the field where the ratio of theory to data was (i) __________: there was an abundance of theories, but almost no data. Recently, however, that ratio has flipped. A huge and ever-increasing amount of data has (ii) __________ all theories but one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　nearly infinite; eliminated
flip
flip: standard unit headword
#124 TC SINGLE
Bridges are often offered as __________ examples of engineering, given that a bridge's structure is out in the open for all to see.Bridges are often offered as __________ examples of engineering, given that a bridge's structure is out in the open for all to see.
答案：A　intelligible
for all
for all: standard unit headword
#125 SE
Any notion of justice in the fortunes of artists is __________: works of equal value and quality produce quite different returns or no returns at all.Any notion of justice in the fortunes of artists is __________: works of equal value and quality produce quite different returns or no returns at all.
答案：A, C　baseless; untenable
return
return: standard unit headword
#126 TC TRIPLE
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
for all
for all: standard unit headword
#127 TC DOUBLE
People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentment if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and imitated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentment if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and imitated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　clandestine; open
resolve
resolve: standard unit headword
#128 TC SINGLE
Despite the fact that some of the company's hiring and promotion practices have been widely __________, these practices still persist in certain divisions of the company.Despite the fact that some of the company's hiring and promotion practices have been widely __________, these practices still persist in certain divisions of the company.
答案：C　proscribed
promotion
promotion: standard unit headword
#129 SE
It is not unusual for American education leaders to hold up another nation as a model for school reform: in the mid-nineteenth century, such figures __________ the professionalism and structure of the Prussian school system.It is not unusual for American education leaders to hold up another nation as a model for school reform: in the mid-nineteenth century, such figures __________ the professionalism and structure of the Prussian school system.
答案：C, E　hailed; acclaimed
acclaim
acclaim: standard unit headword
#130 TC TRIPLE
The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes
promotion
promotion: standard unit headword
#131 TC DOUBLE
Tagore had a sharply defined sense of the (i) __________ of scientific inquiry. The fact that science dealt in statistics and numbers, that its logic was probabilistic, meant that the domain of moral questions (ii) __________ it: moral questions, for Tagore, required certainties, not probabilities.Tagore had a sharply defined sense of the (i) __________ of scientific inquiry. The fact that science dealt in statistics and numbers, that its logic was probabilistic, meant that the domain of moral questions (ii) __________ it: moral questions, for Tagore, required certainties, not probabilities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　limits; lay outside

答案：C　pragmatic

William Perkins, his __________ speaking style notwithstanding, has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.William Perkins, his __________ speaking style notwithstanding, has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.
答案：A, F　fiery; impassioned
notwithstanding

This (ii)__________ is (iii)__________ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)__________ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)__________ is (iii)__________ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　rich; problem; augmented

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　intransigent; uncompromising

答案：D　insurmountable

Blank 2

答案：C　capricious

答案：B, F　capricious; impulsive

Yet when problems are (ii)__________, or require combining varied sources of information, computers are (iii)__________ human intelligence.

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A　a deflationary

答案：D, F　proscriptive; exacting

However, watermills' greater capacity and reliability provided a better (iii)__________ the money required to build the mill.In medieval Europe, watermills were more (i)__________ than windmills. It is true that windmills could be built virtually anywhere, whereas watermills (ii)__________. However, watermills' greater capacity and reliability provided a better (iii)__________ the money required to build the mill.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　profitable; were suitable only for certain locations; return on
return

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　relish; insufferable

#148 TC SINGLE

答案：B　myopia

#149 SE

答案：A, F　short-lived; ephemeral
ephemeral

The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforces

The author argued that the field of sociology has been overly (i) __________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii) __________.The author argued that the field of sociology has been overly (i) __________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　narrow in scope; parochialism
define

#152 TC SINGLE
As a genre that prizes authenticity over artifice, memoir puts a special pressure on literary style: if the memoirist's language tends toward the highly lyrical, it can __________ the memoirist's claim to truth.As a genre that prizes authenticity over artifice, memoir puts a special pressure on literary style: if the memoirist's language tends toward the highly lyrical, it can __________ the memoirist's claim to truth.
答案：C　hinder
expeditehinder

The candidate seeks to depict his opponent as being __________, as one who is simply unable to make a decision and stand his ground.The candidate seeks to depict his opponent as being __________, as one who is simply unable to make a decision and stand his ground.
答案：A, E　inconstant; capricious
ground
ground: standard unit headword
#154 TC TRIPLE
In adolescence, (i)__________ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)__________ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)__________ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)__________ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering
define

#155 TC DOUBLE
The novel's heroine shows a remarkable (i) __________ to worship at the altar of youth; in her world, youth is (ii) __________, while age, by contrast, confers competence and wisdom.The novel's heroine shows a remarkable (i) __________ to worship at the altar of youth; in her world, youth is (ii) __________, while age, by contrast, confers competence and wisdom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　disinclination; callow
confer
confer: standard unit headword
#156 SE
The tribes’ __________ a settled mode of living was derived from their long-standing traditions, which, though differing from one tribal group to another, always included a resistance to nomadic lifestyles.The tribes’ __________ a settled mode of living was derived from their long-standing traditions, which, though differing from one tribal group to another, always included a resistance to nomadic lifestyles.
答案：B, C　proclivity toward; predilection for
proclivity
proclivity: standard unit headword
#157 TC SINGLE
When it comes to cheating in competitive sports and games, chess is a particular case that might seem all but __________, since after all the board and its pieces are out in the open for all to see.When it comes to cheating in competitive sports and games, chess is a particular case that might seem all but __________, since after all the board and its pieces are out in the open for all to see.
答案：A　invulnerable
for all
for all: standard unit headword
#158 TC TRIPLE
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predate that event
return
return: standard unit headword
#159 TC DOUBLE
Historians have been (i) __________ African American fraternal organizations, in part due to the (ii) __________ historical evidence: the relevant records are scarce and difficult to obtain.Historians have been (i) __________ African American fraternal organizations, in part due to the (ii) __________ historical evidence: the relevant records are scarce and difficult to obtain.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　hindered in studying; insufficiency of
hinder
hinder: standard unit headword

答案：C　marginal
notwithstanding
notwithstanding: standard unit headword
#161 SE
Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by __________ some kinds of knowledge as real science.Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by __________ some kinds of knowledge as real science.
答案：B, F　sanctioning; endorsing
dispute
dispute: standard unit headword

Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)__________ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)__________. People reasonably (iii)__________ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)__________ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)__________. People reasonably (iii)__________ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　surfeit; virtue; forgo
forgo
forgo: standard unit headword
#163 TC DOUBLE
Each new generation of students grow up (i) __________ the world of classical physics, with its mostly intuitive, billiard-ball causality; that is the everyday vantage from which we approach the alien world of quantum physics, which has for this reason never lost its air of (ii) __________.Each new generation of students grow up (i) __________ the world of classical physics, with its mostly intuitive, billiard-ball causality; that is the everyday vantage from which we approach the alien world of quantum physics, which has for this reason never lost its air of (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　immersed in; radicalism
air
air: stem: target sense: manner/atmosphere or public expression

The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.
答案：A　verisimilitude
abstraction

答案：B, D　deflate; soothe
reassure

Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　established; defining features of; propose

Blank 2

答案：C　inscrutable

答案：B, D　abstracted from; divorced from

Attacking what he calls the "auteur myth"--the idea of the director as the single purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)__________--he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)__________ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)__________ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii) __________ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii) __________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzy about comics today is (i) __________. After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii) __________ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii) __________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has set in.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　understandable; arousal; breakthrough

Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.

Blank 2
Blank 3

For example, the (ii) __________ Maxwell's equations and Galilean invariance led Einstein to propose the special theory of relativity. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii) __________ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i) __________. Blank 2

Instead, it continues to operate, sometimes under its own name, sometimes under other names. #205 TC TRIPLE
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)__________ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)__________ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)__________.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)__________ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)__________ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory evidence

答案：B, F　inborn; innate

And many of these problems seem to get worse the further back in time we are willing to look.For all of its (i) __________, there are a number of very substantial (ii) __________ that arise from the idea of the big bang. And many of these problems seem to get worse the further back in time we are willing to look.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　verified predictions; puzzles

#208 TC SINGLE
One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are __________ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are __________ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.
答案：B　incommensurable
cosmopolitan

#209 TC TRIPLE
Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work, (i) __________ to propose measures that might (ii) __________ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii) __________, they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work, (i) __________ to propose measures that might (ii) __________ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii) __________, they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　forgoing opportunities; ameliorate; reticence

This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for __________ story.This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for __________ story.
答案：B, E　a diffuse; an amorphous

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　legitimate within; impede

#212 TC TRIPLE
Some scientists speculate that changes in atmospheric methane were responsible for some abrupt global warming events in the past such as the one at the end of the geologic period known as the Younger Dryas. There is good evidence that large quantities of methane have been released quickly in the past through natural processes; however, methane is a relatively (i)__________ molecule in the atmosphere and thus (ii)__________ through chemical reactions with other compounds. The effect of even a large increase would therefore be (iii)__________.Some scientists speculate that changes in atmospheric methane were responsible for some abrupt global warming events in the past such as the one at the end of the geologic period known as the Younger Dryas. There is good evidence that large quantities of methane have been released quickly in the past through natural processes; however, methane is a relatively (i)__________ molecule in the atmosphere and thus (ii)__________ through chemical reactions with other compounds. The effect of even a large increase would therefore be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unstable; fairly quickly destroyed; ephemeral
ephemeral
ephemeral: standard unit headword
#213 TC SINGLE
Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating.Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating.
答案：B　addiction to
vexation
vexation: standard unit headword
#214 SE
Whereas ground-based interferometers detect astrophysical events briefly and infrequently, space-based ones should hear a __________ of signals as soon as they turn on, including a constant chorus from binary white dwarfs in our galaxy.Whereas ground-based interferometers detect astrophysical events briefly and infrequently, space-based ones should hear a __________ of signals as soon as they turn on, including a constant chorus from binary white dwarfs in our galaxy.
答案：B, C　plethora; profusion

On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.High self-esteem has recently assumed a somewhat (i) __________ status in social psychology. On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　paradoxical; antisocial
confer
confer: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　retain; condemnation of; forgo

forgo: standard unit headword
#217 TC SINGLE
If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition, since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition, since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build population from scratch.
答案：B　augmenting

答案：B, E　dogmatic; orthodox

For example, the principal challenge to geological uniformitarianism has been its logical opposite - catastrophism.Humans have a curious (i) __________ problems in terms of (ii) __________, and scientists are no exception. For example, the principal challenge to geological uniformitarianism has been its logical opposite - catastrophism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predilection to define; dichotomies

define: standard unit headword
#220 TC SINGLE
Some ambitious lawyers are willing to work on Supreme Court cases without charge in an effort to gain __________; they believe that this increased cachet will help them succeed in the future.Some ambitious lawyers are willing to work on Supreme Court cases without charge in an effort to gain __________; they believe that this increased cachet will help them succeed in the future.
答案：C　prestige

#221 TC TRIPLE
The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility
perennial
perennial: standard unit headword
#222 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong--and, consequently, we are lacking in any __________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong--and, consequently, we are lacking in any __________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：A, C　incisive; trenchant

Along with the many testimonials to journalistic courage and a memorial to journalists who lost their lives on the job, the museum presents noteworthy examples of journalistic (ii) __________.The Newseum is a museum created by, for, and about journalists and newshounds. But for all its celebration of the news industry, its creators have taken care to avoid (i) __________. Along with the many testimonials to journalistic courage and a memorial to journalists who lost their lives on the job, the museum presents noteworthy examples of journalistic (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　puffery; malfeasance

答案：B　bemoaned

The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)__________ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)__________, for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)__________.The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)__________ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)__________, for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　spontaneity; immediacy; improvisation

air: stem: target sense: manner/atmosphere or public expression

Newman and Principe suggest that a third term, "chymistry," should be used to denote the transitional early modern phase when "alchemy" and "chemistry" were becoming (iii)__________.William Newman and Lawrence Principe point out that prior to the seventeenth century the terms "alchemy" and "chemistry" were largely (i)__________ in European usage. Blank 2

#261 TC TRIPLE
It is true that the cost of human-based space infrastructures, such as the International Space Station (ISS), is much higher than the cost of unmanned missions. However, the (i) __________ such projects (ii) __________ their (iii) __________. The ISS is a major accomplishment for all countries involved: it is the largest on-orbit structure ever built as well as the largest multinational cooperative project in history.It is true that the cost of human-based space infrastructures, such as the International Space Station (ISS), is much higher than the cost of unmanned missions. However, the (i) __________ such projects (ii) __________ their (iii) __________. The ISS is a major accomplishment for all countries involved: it is the largest on-orbit structure ever built as well as the largest multinational cooperative project in history.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　benefits of; reach beyond; scientific output
for all
for all: standard unit headword
#262 SE
In contrast to the novel's scenic realism and precise characterization is its persistent philosophical __________.In contrast to the novel's scenic realism and precise characterization is its persistent philosophical __________.
答案：B, C　abstraction; generality
abstraction
abstraction: standard unit headword
#263 TC DOUBLE
Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)__________ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)__________ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)__________ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)__________ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　a significant struggle; entails
resolve
resolve: standard unit headword
#264 TC SINGLE
The best argument in favor of one space after a period over two is __________ one: one space is simpler, cleaner, and more visually pleasing.The best argument in favor of one space after a period over two is __________ one: one space is simpler, cleaner, and more visually pleasing.
答案：A　an aesthetic
pragmatic
pragmatic: standard unit headword
#265 TC TRIPLE
There is a powerful tradition within French writing that has (i)__________ the bounds of conventional morality, (ii)__________ the dictates of Enlightenment reason, and expressed (iii)__________ violence. It is as if the culture that, perhaps more strongly than any other, celebrated reason and geometrical order, also provoked within itself a deep, wild, and willfully primitive reaction, a return of the repressed par excellence.There is a powerful tradition within French writing that has (i)__________ the bounds of conventional morality, (ii)__________ the dictates of Enlightenment reason, and expressed (iii)__________ violence. It is as if the culture that, perhaps more strongly than any other, celebrated reason and geometrical order, also provoked within itself a deep, wild, and willfully primitive reaction, a return of the repressed par excellence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　challenged; defied; an abiding fascination with
return
return: standard unit headword
#266 SE
Although the philosopher frequently dealt with __________ theoretical topics, she was capable of writing in a style that was comprehensible to a general audience.Although the philosopher frequently dealt with __________ theoretical topics, she was capable of writing in a style that was comprehensible to a general audience.
答案：B, E　abstruse; recondite
abstruse
abstruse: standard unit headword
#267 TC DOUBLE
The national bank has been uncommonly powerful in comparison to its counterparts in other nations. It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation's banking system is (i) __________ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii) __________.The national bank has been uncommonly powerful in comparison to its counterparts in other nations. It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation's banking system is (i) __________ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　unencumbered by; discretionary
hinder
hinder: standard unit headword
#268 TC SINGLE
The most pervasive myth about mathematics is that the logical structure of mathematics is __________, that logic captures the essence of the subject.The most pervasive myth about mathematics is that the logical structure of mathematics is __________, that logic captures the essence of the subject.
答案：A　definitive
abstruse
abstruse: standard unit headword
#269 TC TRIPLE
Some critics say that the prime minister (i) __________ her positions too easily, but her supporters claim that she is merely (ii) __________ rather than quixotic and adaptable rather than (iii) __________.Some critics say that the prime minister (i) __________ her positions too easily, but her supporters claim that she is merely (ii) __________ rather than quixotic and adaptable rather than (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　changes; pragmatic; capricious
pragmatic
pragmatic: standard unit headword
#270 SE
In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly __________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly __________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.
答案：B, E　static; unvarying
entity
entity: standard unit headword
#271 TC DOUBLE
Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　an anonymous; confessional
scathing
scathing: standard unit headword
#272 SE
Aerial viewings of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechuan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appears crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.Aerial viewings of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechuan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appears crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.
答案：A, C　reveal; manifest

#273 TC SINGLE
A beaver's daily activity rhythm can be __________ the natural 24-hour light cycle; indeed some beaver colonies in Canada have shown a free-running cycle of activity that deviates considerably from 24 hours.A beaver's daily activity rhythm can be __________ the natural 24-hour light cycle; indeed some beaver colonies in Canada have shown a free-running cycle of activity that deviates considerably from 24 hours.
答案：B　decoupled from

define: standard unit headword
#274 TC TRIPLE
One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)__________. This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)__________, even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)__________. Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)__________. This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)__________, even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)__________. Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　isolated; negligible; cause for concern
spate
spate: standard unit headword
#275 TC DOUBLE
Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i) __________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudoscience, much of the writing has a (ii) __________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i) __________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudoscience, much of the writing has a (ii) __________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　inadequacies; debunking

abstraction: standard unit headword
