Within the culture as a whole, natural science has been so successful that the word scientific is used in (i)__________ manner; it is often assumed that to call something “scientific” is to imply that its reliability has been (ii)__________ by research whose results cannot reasonably be (iii)__________.Within the culture as a whole, natural science has been so successful that the word scientific is used in (i)__________ manner; it is often assumed that to call something “scientific” is to imply that its reliability has been (ii)__________ by research whose results cannot reasonably be (iii)__________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　an honorific; established; disputed

答案：D　uncomfortable precedent

reassure: standard unit headword
#278 SE
Ann Larson decided that her new play, Antagonism, would explore the notion of forgiveness and, indeed, the most remarkable aspect of the play-its title notwithstanding-may be the possibility of some kind of __________ between people.Ann Larson decided that her new play, Antagonism, would explore the notion of forgiveness and, indeed, the most remarkable aspect of the play-its title notwithstanding-may be the possibility of some kind of __________ between people.
答案：B, E　reconciliation; rapprochement
notwithstanding
notwithstanding: standard unit headword

Yet they shouldn't even be able to get off the ground, at least not according to a straightforward interpretation of the laws of aerodynamics.Insects are often (i) __________ as the world's most versatile and (ii) __________ flying machines. Yet they shouldn't even be able to get off the ground, at least not according to a straightforward interpretation of the laws of aerodynamics.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　touted; maneuverable

答案：C　recondite

The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inadequate; secondhand

The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained

#289 TC TRIPLE
Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific works validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific works validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed

答案：D, F　fanciful; chimerical

But this agricultural archive is (i) __________ as a result of war, storms, scant money, and bad management, particularly in the world's most (ii) __________ places.

The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　accelerated; at the same rate; self-evident

Of course children like sweetener, which is (ii) __________ to sellers, since children's taste will not change once they are used to a certain brand.

Frequently that time happened in a great classroom somewhere, in the hands of the one (ii) __________ teacher we can each remember.Many of us remember a time when discovery and the thrill of learning were forces in our lives and were (i) __________. Frequently that time happened in a great classroom somewhere, in the hands of the one (ii) __________ teacher we can each remember.

#309 TC TRIPLE
History is about recognizing continuities and therefore the historian must (i) __________ stories that rope together the disparate worlds of past and present. But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. It is the experience of discontinuity that alerts us to the (iii) __________ of the past and sets us looking for narratives to capture that unfamiliar entity.History is about recognizing continuities and therefore the historian must (i) __________ stories that rope together the disparate worlds of past and present. But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. It is the experience of discontinuity that alerts us to the (iii) __________ of the past and sets us looking for narratives to capture that unfamiliar entity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　weave; interruptedness; strangeness

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　inversely related; unfeeling
ephemeral
ephemeral: standard unit headword
#312 TC TRIPLE
Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored
ground
ground: standard unit headword
#313 SE
Shifting Fortunes take a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries: it is a chronologically and thematically __________ study and nothing more.Shifting Fortunes take a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries: it is a chronologically and thematically __________ study and nothing more.
答案：B, E　confined; circumscribed
taxing
taxing: standard unit headword
#314 TC DOUBLE
Seemingly to compensate for the (i) __________ nature of her subject, in her review the theater critic (ii) __________ a casual chatty style.Seemingly to compensate for the (i) __________ nature of her subject, in her review the theater critic (ii) __________ a casual chatty style.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rarefied; opted for
define
define: standard unit headword
#315 TC TRIPLE
Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　invariably face opposition; accustomed; militate against
return
return: standard unit headword
#316 SE
Although its director __________ that the movie uses a documentary approach in portraying the famous sit-down strike, in practice its characters are heavily fictionalized and fall into familiar Hollywood types.Although its director __________ that the movie uses a documentary approach in portraying the famous sit-down strike, in practice its characters are heavily fictionalized and fall into familiar Hollywood types.
答案：A, F　asserts; maintains
dispute
dispute: standard unit headword
#317 TC DOUBLE
Because scientific breakthroughs tend to occur when a prevailing theory's predictions start to fail, scientists sometimes find it (i) __________ when a theory persists--as the standard model of particle physics has done--in making (ii) __________ predictions.Because scientific breakthroughs tend to occur when a prevailing theory's predictions start to fail, scientists sometimes find it (i) __________ when a theory persists--as the standard model of particle physics has done--in making (ii) __________ predictions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　frustrating; correct
abstract
abstract: standard unit headword
#318 TC TRIPLE
Concerned that her new colleague would not approve of her political opinions, the reporter briefly considered (i) __________. But she quickly resolved that she would not (ii) __________ her personal views merely for the sake of (iii) __________.Concerned that her new colleague would not approve of her political opinions, the reporter briefly considered (i) __________. But she quickly resolved that she would not (ii) __________ her personal views merely for the sake of (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　prevarication; suppress; concord
resolve
resolve: standard unit headword
#319 SE
Even if the story now seems a surprisingly innocuous overture to the author’s later, more fully developed narrations, it __________ some of the key traits of those bleaker tales.Even if the story now seems a surprisingly innocuous overture to the author’s later, more fully developed narrations, it __________ some of the key traits of those bleaker tales.
答案：D, F　anticipates; prefigures
undercut
undercut: standard unit headword
#320 TC DOUBLE
Readers had little reason to (i) __________ what proved to be the rather uncanny (ii) __________ of Kaja Silverman's and bell hooks's polemics, given the two authors' contrary methodologies, interests, and audiences.Readers had little reason to (i) __________ what proved to be the rather uncanny (ii) __________ of Kaja Silverman's and bell hooks's polemics, given the two authors' contrary methodologies, interests, and audiences.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　anticipate; convergence
dispute
dispute: standard unit headword
#321 TC TRIPLE
While the subtropical ocean gyres are (i) __________ accumulation zones of floating plastic debris, the possibility of plastic accumulation at polar latitudes has been (ii) __________ because of the (iii) __________ of nearby pollution sources.While the subtropical ocean gyres are (i) __________ accumulation zones of floating plastic debris, the possibility of plastic accumulation at polar latitudes has been (ii) __________ because of the (iii) __________ of nearby pollution sources.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　recognized as; overlooked; dearth
plastic
plastic: standard unit headword
#322 SE
Benjamin Franklin’s reputation is so much one of approaching scientific investigation with common sense empiricism that it is somewhat startling to realize how __________ the great experimenter’s mentality truly was.Benjamin Franklin’s reputation is so much one of approaching scientific investigation with common sense empiricism that it is somewhat startling to realize how __________ the great experimenter’s mentality truly was.
答案：B, D　speculative; conjectural
pragmatic
pragmatic: standard unit headword
#323 TC DOUBLE
In a reflection of the senator's enduring popularity, the articles written in response to her impending retirement were less a (i) __________ evaluation than a (ii) __________ summary of her career.In a reflection of the senator's enduring popularity, the articles written in response to her impending retirement were less a (i) __________ evaluation than a (ii) __________ summary of her career.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　valedictory; glowing
scathing
scathing: standard unit headword
#324 TC TRIPLE
The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　inherit; tend to embody; resist
define
define: standard unit headword
#325 SE
Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could __________ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could __________ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.
答案：B, E　galvanize; animate
presume
presume: standard unit headword
#326 TC DOUBLE
Sinden notes that in the face of warnings about the perils of climate change, the need to agree on strategies for reducing greenhouse-gas emissions is increasingly urgent. The question of who should reduce emissions is not (i) __________. Rather, according to Sinden, it is the question of who should bear the costs of these reductions that is (ii) __________.Sinden notes that in the face of warnings about the perils of climate change, the need to agree on strategies for reducing greenhouse-gas emissions is increasingly urgent. The question of who should reduce emissions is not (i) __________. Rather, according to Sinden, it is the question of who should bear the costs of these reductions that is (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　particularly vexed; contentious
resolve
resolve: standard unit headword
#327 TC TRIPLE
It has been suggested that the use of metaphors from the realms of battle and sports to describe arguments contributes to the (i) __________ of the argumentative process. Such metaphors may (ii) __________ adversarial conceptions of argument, leading interlocutors to behave aggressively in verbal disputes, thereby (iii) __________ a process that ideally leads to resolution.It has been suggested that the use of metaphors from the realms of battle and sports to describe arguments contributes to the (i) __________ of the argumentative process. Such metaphors may (ii) __________ adversarial conceptions of argument, leading interlocutors to behave aggressively in verbal disputes, thereby (iii) __________ a process that ideally leads to resolution.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　breakdown; promote; undermining
dispute
dispute: standard unit headword
#328 SE
Today, the chair of the task force in charge of revising psychiatry diagnostic manual is a __________ post – people work for years to position themselves as candidates – but in the early nineteen, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.Today, the chair of the task force in charge of revising psychiatry diagnostic manual is a __________ post – people work for years to position themselves as candidates – but in the early nineteen, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.
答案：A, E　a coveted; an enviable
taxing
taxing: standard unit headword
#329 TC DOUBLE
Lohr's (i) __________ the trappings of literary celebrity creates a Romantic aura for him: by distancing himself from all public discourse about himself or his work, Lohr becomes an even greater, albeit more (ii) __________, celebrity than most authors manage in all their interviews and memoirs.Lohr's (i) __________ the trappings of literary celebrity creates a Romantic aura for him: by distancing himself from all public discourse about himself or his work, Lohr becomes an even greater, albeit more (ii) __________, celebrity than most authors manage in all their interviews and memoirs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　refusal to wear; mysterious
renowned
renowned: standard unit headword
#330 TC TRIPLE
Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　churlish; approbation; consistently perspicacious
for all
for all: standard unit headword
#331 SE
An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematical formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematical formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.
答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar
interdependent
interdependent: standard unit headword

Their (ii) __________ the manifestations of modern science--railroads, radio, airplanes, and so forth--was potently expressed in language that often bordered on the mystical and magical.Intent on molding backward and agrarian Russia into a modern state, the architects of the Russian Revolution (i) __________ the transformative powers of science and technology. Their (ii) __________ the manifestations of modern science--railroads, radio, airplanes, and so forth--was potently expressed in language that often bordered on the mystical and magical.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　extolled; collective infatuation with

#333 TC TRIPLE
The (i)__________ nature of the candidate’s comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as a radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)__________ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)__________ leader.The (i)__________ nature of the candidate’s comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as a radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)__________ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)__________ leader.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　platitudinous; innocuous; conciliatory

Apparent flaws in the sculptor’s work have not __________ its respectful reception by most modern critics.Apparent flaws in the sculptor’s work have not __________ its respectful reception by most modern critics.
答案：D, F　prevented; precluded

overshadow: standard unit headword
#335 TC DOUBLE
When an organism is functioning normally, excessive fat is stored in adipose tissue. Thus, the (i) __________ of such tissue may be (ii) __________: the fat is diverted to other organs, such as the liver and the heart, causing serious health problems.When an organism is functioning normally, excessive fat is stored in adipose tissue. Thus, the (i) __________ of such tissue may be (ii) __________: the fat is diverted to other organs, such as the liver and the heart, causing serious health problems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　dearth; hazardous

ephemeral: standard unit headword
#336 SE
Of all the singer’s works, this album is the most dependent on the musical conventions of her day; it was both the least __________ of her albums and the most commercially successful.Of all the singer’s works, this album is the most dependent on the musical conventions of her day; it was both the least __________ of her albums and the most commercially successful.
答案：C, D　experimental; innovative

acclaim: standard unit headword
#337 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
tweak
tweak: standard unit headword

Because human emotions are behavioral complexes, they presumably took a very long time to develop. Therefore, a complete lack of any (i) __________, human emotions in ancestral species is (ii) __________.Because human emotions are behavioral complexes, they presumably took a very long time to develop. Therefore, a complete lack of any (i) __________, human emotions in ancestral species is (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　antecedents of; improbable
reassure
reassure: standard unit headword
#339 SE
Williams finds the appearance of Whitman’s Leaves of Grass in 1855 nearly __________ given the immense disparity between Whitman’s earlier published works, which Williams finds dismal, and the consummate mastery of Leaves.Williams finds the appearance of Whitman’s Leaves of Grass in 1855 nearly __________ given the immense disparity between Whitman’s earlier published works, which Williams finds dismal, and the consummate mastery of Leaves.
答案：D, F　inexplicable; incomprehensible

Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.

答案：A, D, G　restrain; incursions of; corrupt

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　esoteric; parochial

答案：B, D　a cumbersome; an unwieldy

When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the establish view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the establish view.

Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i) __________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii) __________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i) __________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii) __________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　didactic; amount to

mock: standard unit headword

Well organized and researched and including all significant discoveries of medical scientists, the History of Western Medicines has been justly called __________.Well organized and researched and including all significant discoveries of medical scientists, the History of Western Medicines has been justly called __________.
答案：A, C　encyclopedic; exhaustive
rambling
rambling: standard unit headword
#346 TC TRIPLE
The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　get; incidental to; powerful antidote to
ground
ground: standard unit headword

The lecturer (i) __________ to provide innovative insights into important issues, but her promise of originality was more than she delivered: most of her pronouncements were (ii) __________.The lecturer (i) __________ to provide innovative insights into important issues, but her promise of originality was more than she delivered: most of her pronouncements were (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undertook; platitudinous
trenchant
trenchant: standard unit headword

As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her __________; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her __________; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.
答案：B, D　humility; modesty
integrity
integrity: standard unit headword

Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from

overshadow: standard unit headword
#350 TC DOUBLE
A list is at once (i) __________ and (ii) __________; in other words, a list is simultaneously the sum of the items on it and those individual (and often unrelated) items themselves.A list is at once (i) __________ and (ii) __________; in other words, a list is simultaneously the sum of the items on it and those individual (and often unrelated) items themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　accretive; discontinuous

pragmatic: standard unit headword
#351 SE
While normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the growth of crops, recently there has been an increase in the frequency of high-intensity floods that do not have such welcome effects.While normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the growth of crops, recently there has been an increase in the frequency of high-intensity floods that do not have such welcome effects.
答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable to
hinder
hinder: standard unit headword
#352 TC TRIPLE
Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious
insurmountable
insurmountable: standard unit headword
#353 TC DOUBLE
When the musician was fighting for the organization he heads to be taken seriously, his forceful pronouncements were regarded as (i) __________ by many in the jazz community. By now, however, his statements have taken on a different tone, becoming more (ii) __________, and consequently they are perceived as being more directed at coalition building.When the musician was fighting for the organization he heads to be taken seriously, his forceful pronouncements were regarded as (i) __________ by many in the jazz community. By now, however, his statements have taken on a different tone, becoming more (ii) __________, and consequently they are perceived as being more directed at coalition building.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　divisive; temperate
extroverted
extroverted: standard unit headword

Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had __________ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had __________ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.
答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable
studied
studied: standard unit headword
#355 TC TRIPLE
The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)__________ and (ii)__________, prove one of two conclusions; their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)__________, or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)__________ and (ii)__________, prove one of two conclusions; their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)__________, or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　expensive; useless; serve no practical purpose

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　mistaking; effect

hinder: standard unit headword

答案：E, F　mitigated; abated

#362 TC DOUBLE
Shark teeth are among the most common of all vertebrate fossils. While the abundance of teeth may (i) __________ creatures renowned for their voracious appetites, it contrasts sharply with the (ii) __________ of shark skeletons in the fossil record.Shark teeth are among the most common of all vertebrate fossils. While the abundance of teeth may (i) __________ creatures renowned for their voracious appetites, it contrasts sharply with the (ii) __________ of shark skeletons in the fossil record.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　befit; rarity
renownedintegrity
renowned: standard unit headword | integrity: standard unit headword
#363 SE
She expected her book to be __________, but in fact few of her readers disagreed with its premise that street art, long considered a mere sideshow entertainment, deserved to be regarded as high art.She expected her book to be __________, but in fact few of her readers disagreed with its premise that street art, long considered a mere sideshow entertainment, deserved to be regarded as high art.
答案：A, E　controversial; provocative
acclaim
acclaim: standard unit headword

The (i)__________ nature of the candidate's comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)__________ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)__________ leader.The (i)__________ nature of the candidate's comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)__________ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)__________ leader.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　platitudinous; innocuous; conciliatory
truculent
truculent: standard unit headword
#365 TC DOUBLE
The explanation of tornado formation according to which tornadoes form during a twisting clash of air masses is clearly (i) __________: such clashes occur very frequently both in the United States and in countries where tornadoes are (ii) __________.The explanation of tornado formation according to which tornadoes form during a twisting clash of air masses is clearly (i) __________: such clashes occur very frequently both in the United States and in countries where tornadoes are (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　oversimplified; virtually unknown
airstudied
studied: standard unit headword
#366 SE
Today the chair of task force in charge of revising the psychiatric diagnostic manual is __________ post—people work for years to position themselves as candidates—but in the early 1970s, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.Today the chair of task force in charge of revising the psychiatric diagnostic manual is __________ post—people work for years to position themselves as candidates—but in the early 1970s, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.
答案：A, E　a coveted; an enviable
taxing
taxing: standard unit headword
#367 TC TRIPLE
Schechter is atypically (i) __________ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii) __________ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii) __________ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.Schechter is atypically (i) __________ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii) __________ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii) __________ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unimpressed with; terrifying; a commonplace
enamor
enamor: standard unit headword
#368 SE
For parents, the pleasure of letting children choose which book to read aloud together is not always __________: I well remembered my inner groans when my child would constantly pick my least favorite book from the shelf.For parents, the pleasure of letting children choose which book to read aloud together is not always __________: I well remembered my inner groans when my child would constantly pick my least favorite book from the shelf.
答案：D, F　unalloyed; unqualified

Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)__________ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)__________, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　dogmatic; qualify; plentiful

#370 SE
Shifting Fortunes takes a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth century; it is a chronologically and thematically __________ study and nothing more.Shifting Fortunes takes a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth century; it is a chronologically and thematically __________ study and nothing more.
答案：C, E　circumscribed; confined

#371 TC TRIPLE
Part of what currently makes it so (i)__________ to arrive at a scientific understanding of the living world is that while technological advances have produced a cascade of data - from detailed genome sequence to the sophisticated satellite imagery that documents the planet's ecosystems - our ability to (ii)__________ these data still lags far behind their (iii)__________.Part of what currently makes it so (i)__________ to arrive at a scientific understanding of the living world is that while technological advances have produced a cascade of data - from detailed genome sequence to the sophisticated satellite imagery that documents the planet's ecosystems - our ability to (ii)__________ these data still lags far behind their (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　challenging; apprehend; acquisition

dispute: standard unit headword
#372 SE
Although the essayist’s arguments did not __________ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.Although the essayist’s arguments did not __________ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.
答案：B, E　confound; perplex

This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author's choice of format, which is the (i)__________ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)__________ reception that academics - motivated partly by (iii)__________ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards - generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author's choice of format, which is the (i)__________ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)__________ reception that academics - motivated partly by (iii)__________ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards - generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　inverse; scornful; snobbish elitism

integrity: standard unit headword

In order to cultivate new repertoire, the music industry is providing a hearing for previously __________ composers.In order to cultivate new repertoire, the music industry is providing a hearing for previously __________ composers.
答案：E, F　anonymous; obscure

renowned: standard unit headword
#375 TC TRIPLE
The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebos often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebos often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　get; incidental to; powerful antidote to
ground
ground: standard unit headword
#376 SE
For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy __________; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy __________; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.
答案：E, F　simplicity; intelligibility

A new sense of (ii)__________ came across: a sense that the world was in (iii)__________, that life for one's children and grandchildren would be very different, all because of this wondrous force and its uses.

答案：C, E, G　exhilaration; futurity; a state of change

答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable for

答案：B, D　constraining; deterring

答案：D, E　alarm; unsettle

#387 TC TRIPLE
The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)__________, a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)__________ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)__________ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)__________, a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)__________ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)__________ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deferential; radical; profound shift
resolve
resolve: standard unit headword
#388 SE
As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her __________: rather than presenting fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her __________: rather than presenting fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.
答案：B, D　humility; modesty
integrity

#389 TC TRIPLE
One of the problems with recent books that perpetuate the view of Jane Austen as moral tutor, a sort of Miss Manners for the ages, is that they understand manners as (i)__________, as near-universal and timeless behavioral ideals or, worse still, a set of rules to be followed. It is crucial, however, to (ii)__________. In other words, one needs to resist (iii)__________ generic "manners" with more linguistically and historically precise designations, not least because Austen herself distinguishes.One of the problems with recent books that perpetuate the view of Jane Austen as moral tutor, a sort of Miss Manners for the ages, is that they understand manners as (i)__________, as near-universal and timeless behavioral ideals or, worse still, a set of rules to be followed. It is crucial, however, to (ii)__________. In other words, one needs to resist (iii)__________ generic "manners" with more linguistically and historically precise designations, not least because Austen herself distinguishes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　monolithic; differentiate various behaviors; conflating
context
context: standard unit headword
#390 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：D, F　cursory; perfunctory

Describing cosmopolitanism and provincialism as (i) __________ concepts misses part of their relationship. They are actually (ii) __________, both in the strictly semiotic sense that the idea of cosmopolitanism is (iii) __________ unless we can oppose it to something like provincialism and in a more practical sense: much of cosmopolitanism involves recombining elements of provincial cultures.Describing cosmopolitanism and provincialism as (i) __________ concepts misses part of their relationship. They are actually (ii) __________, both in the strictly semiotic sense that the idea of cosmopolitanism is (iii) __________ unless we can oppose it to something like provincialism and in a more practical sense: much of cosmopolitanism involves recombining elements of provincial cultures.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　antithetical; mutually constitutive; meaningless

答案：B, F　shortcoming; fault

No matter how much input orchestra players are allowed in choosing a music director, they never feel they have enough. Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　carping; disgruntlement regarding; preposterous

答案：B, F　obtain; glean

Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) __________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) __________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　originality; avant-garde; abandon

abstract: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　indifference; apathy

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　did a great disservice to; nullified; begotten

答案：A, C　irony; incongruity

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　obscure the paucity; recondite; disguise

In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.
答案：B, D　haphazard; arbitrary

The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) __________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) __________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) __________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) __________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) __________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) __________ as time passes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading

答案：A, C　sparse; paltry

#405 TC TRIPLE
Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) __________ even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) __________ even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　expansion; distrust; grudging
pragmatic
pragmatic: standard unit headword

Although Gwendolyn Brooks's work is, on the whole, __________, in some earlier works, intense linguistic and semantic compression occasionally present minor difficulties.Although Gwendolyn Brooks's work is, on the whole, __________, in some earlier works, intense linguistic and semantic compression occasionally present minor difficulties.
答案：A, B　rarely inaccessible; fairly comprehensible

#407 TC TRIPLE
It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　exaggerated expectations; realism; preclude
presume
presume: standard unit headword
#408 SE
Although oceanic water currents can appear to be almost completely __________, scientists recognize the existence of invisible separators, which they call transport barriers, and these maintain a measure of order and structure in the chaotic flow.Although oceanic water currents can appear to be almost completely __________, scientists recognize the existence of invisible separators, which they call transport barriers, and these maintain a measure of order and structure in the chaotic flow.
答案：A, F　uncontrolled; random

#409 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
insurmountable
insurmountable: standard unit headword
#410 SE
Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.
答案：D, F　prosaic; pedestrian
frivolous
frivolous: standard unit headword
#411 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
studied
studied: standard unit headword
#412 SE
Scholars viewed the ancient Antikythera mechanism as __________ until scientific imaging methods revealed inscriptions on it showing that its purpose was to calculate the locations and movements of the stars, Moon, and planets.Scholars viewed the ancient Antikythera mechanism as __________ until scientific imaging methods revealed inscriptions on it showing that its purpose was to calculate the locations and movements of the stars, Moon, and planets.
答案：B, E　cryptic; enigmatic
enlighten
enlighten: standard unit headword
#413 TC TRIPLE
One of the remarkable things about (i) __________ is that it can seem so (ii) __________: many of the issues about which people (iii) __________ seem, to the nonzealot, to be gray and ambiguous at the core.One of the remarkable things about (i) __________ is that it can seem so (ii) __________: many of the issues about which people (iii) __________ seem, to the nonzealot, to be gray and ambiguous at the core.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　conviction; unreasonable; feel absolute certainty
duplicity
duplicity: standard unit headword
#414 SE
Ultimately, according to Zuger, all medical advice is __________: it is educated guesswork, perhaps, but guesswork all the same.Ultimately, according to Zuger, all medical advice is __________: it is educated guesswork, perhaps, but guesswork all the same.
答案：B, E　speculative; conjectural
pragmatic
pragmatic: standard unit headword
#415 TC TRIPLE
While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　merely fortuitous; adept at; spurious
abstract
abstract: standard unit headword
#416 SE
With their __________ energy production, massive stars create harsh environments for planetary genesis and may inhibit planet formation altogether: their intense radiation can readily destroy planetary birth sites before new worlds can be constructed.With their __________ energy production, massive stars create harsh environments for planetary genesis and may inhibit planet formation altogether: their intense radiation can readily destroy planetary birth sites before new worlds can be constructed.
答案：B, D　profligate; extravagant
mercurial
mercurial: standard unit headword
#417 TC TRIPLE
For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　unorthodox; aloof from; strongly ahistorical
oracular
oracular: standard unit headword
#418 SE
Barker distrusts traditional poetry because she sees it as inextricably linked to elitism; her version of populism entails a view of poetry as __________ only when it is widely accessible.Barker distrusts traditional poetry because she sees it as inextricably linked to elitism; her version of populism entails a view of poetry as __________ only when it is widely accessible.
答案：B, E　legitimate; justifiable
cosmopolitan
cosmopolitan: standard unit headword
#419 TC TRIPLE
Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　an impersonal; irrelevant to; inseparable from
abstruse
abstruse: standard unit headword
#420 SE
Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.
答案：B, F　challenged; flouted
hinder
hinder: standard unit headword
#421 TC TRIPLE
There can be little question that by the fourteenth century the capacity to (i) __________ a doubtless restricted written Latin was available in virtually all English communities. It would, of course, be (ii) __________ to suppose that all the wealth of material available in Latin since 1100 was put to service in the cultural formation of every writer of business Latin in provincial England, but even schoolbooks (iii) __________ some elements of classical education. Latin literacy was not, in these circumstances, exclusive.There can be little question that by the fourteenth century the capacity to (i) __________ a doubtless restricted written Latin was available in virtually all English communities. It would, of course, be (ii) __________ to suppose that all the wealth of material available in Latin since 1100 was put to service in the cultural formation of every writer of business Latin in provincial England, but even schoolbooks (iii) __________ some elements of classical education. Latin literacy was not, in these circumstances, exclusive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deploy; fanciful; purveyed
forgo
forgo: standard unit headword
#422 SE
In the realm of science and mathematics, ambiguity is generally seen as something that arises from erroneous understanding and must be overcome, yet ambiguity has often __________ developments in science and mathematics.In the realm of science and mathematics, ambiguity is generally seen as something that arises from erroneous understanding and must be overcome, yet ambiguity has often __________ developments in science and mathematics.
答案：C, F　stimulated; spurred
hinder
hinder: standard unit headword
#423 TC TRIPLE
For a variety of reasons that are still difficult to understand fully, Ralph Ellison's masterful short stories have never been (i) __________ until now. This made it almost (ii) __________ to use them in the classroom and hard to use them for scholarly purposes, since many of these stories were only ever published in obscure journals that have been (iii) __________ for quite some time.For a variety of reasons that are still difficult to understand fully, Ralph Ellison's masterful short stories have never been (i) __________ until now. This made it almost (ii) __________ to use them in the classroom and hard to use them for scholarly purposes, since many of these stories were only ever published in obscure journals that have been (iii) __________ for quite some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　collected; impossible; defunct

studied: standard unit headword
#424 SE
Critics have universally recognized Toni Morrison for __________ the limited perspectives of mainstream United States history by reclaiming the narratives of African American history, particularly from a female point of view.Critics have universally recognized Toni Morrison for __________ the limited perspectives of mainstream United States history by reclaiming the narratives of African American history, particularly from a female point of view.
答案：B, F　correcting; redressing
define
define: standard unit headword
#425 TC TRIPLE
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
congestionairoutrage
congestion: standard unit headword | outrage: standard unit headword
#426 SE
Although automated agricultural technology boosts yields and profits, its high degree of prescriptiveness diminishes the role of individual farmers by making their __________ almost irrelevant.Although automated agricultural technology boosts yields and profits, its high degree of prescriptiveness diminishes the role of individual farmers by making their __________ almost irrelevant.
答案：C, E　judgment; discretion
integrity
integrity: standard unit headword
#427 TC TRIPLE
While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)__________ the increasingly (ii)__________ challenges that nature presents. They can, however, make it more (iii)__________ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleague.While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)__________ the increasingly (ii)__________ challenges that nature presents. They can, however, make it more (iii)__________ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleague.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　meeting; difficult; challenging

pragmatic: standard unit headword | abstraction: standard unit headword
#428 SE
Initially portrayed as resistant to change, the protagonist gradually becomes more open-minded; nevertheless, she remains somewhat __________.Initially portrayed as resistant to change, the protagonist gradually becomes more open-minded; nevertheless, she remains somewhat __________.
答案：A, C　cautious; circumspect
flighty
flighty: standard unit headword
#429 TC TRIPLE
How does one evaluate the effects of modern democracy on individuals? The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.How does one evaluate the effects of modern democracy on individuals? The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　mislead; changes for; radically different from

答案：D, F　prevented; precluded

They can, however, make it more (iii)__________ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleagues.While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)__________ the increasingly (ii)__________ challenges that nature presents. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　meeting; difficult; challenging

#432 TC TRIPLE
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique
ephemeralstudied
ephemeral: standard unit headword | studied: standard unit headword
#433 SE
The fierce drought that is gripping the area--and imminent rationing and steep water price increases--is __________ the area's deep economic divide, as wealthy communities guzzle water while poorer communities nearby conserve by necessity.The fierce drought that is gripping the area--and imminent rationing and steep water price increases--is __________ the area's deep economic divide, as wealthy communities guzzle water while poorer communities nearby conserve by necessity.
答案：C, E　highlighting; underscoring

答案：A　uncommon

Garte claims that, as a species, humans are __________ certain types of air pollutants; indeed, humans have been known to survive relatively well in regions where other species have died off because of poor air quality.Garte claims that, as a species, humans are __________ certain types of air pollutants; indeed, humans have been known to survive relatively well in regions where other species have died off because of poor air quality.
答案：C　insensitive to

答案：B　ungainly
